title: George lands SA Masters Champs 2026 hosting privilege
description: GEORGE NEWS - George has been confirmed as...
bodyText:
GEORGE NEWS - George has been confirmed as the host city for the 41st South African Masters Swimming Championship, taking place from 11 to 14 March.


> The event, presented by the Fish Eagle Masters Swimming Club in partnership with the George Municipality, Eden Aquatics and several private sponsors, is expected to draw masters swimmers from across South Africa and neighbouring countries.


The SA Masters Championship caters for adults of varying age groups, with participants ranging from recent school leavers to swimmers over 80.

According to the organisers, George was selected for its established sporting infrastructure and its capacity to host large multi-day events. The municipality has endorsed the championships under its George Naturally brand, which promotes the city as a destination that integrates sport, tourism and natural attractions.

Municipal representatives say hosting the event forms part of the city’s drive to support sports tourism and broaden George’s national profile. In addition to competing, visiting swimmers and supporters are expected to make use of local accommodation, restaurants and tourist sites, contributing to the regional economy.

The championships will be staged at world-class aquatic facilities in George, supported by volunteers and technical officials from clubs across the region.

Further event information is available via email to [email protected].

‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
